errors: [The "ProcedureLookBack" function uses a 1-month lookback period for colonoscopy screening, which is clinically unreasonable. Colonoscopy screening guidelines typically recommend intervals of 10 years for average-risk patients. A 1-month lookback would fail to identify patients due for screening and could inappropriately recommend repeat procedures too soon. The "Colonoscopy" expression checks for zero procedures in this short window, which does not align with standard screening intervals.]

["Minimum Age threshold of 25 years may not align with standard hypertension screening guidelines (e.g., USPSTF recommends screening adults ≥18 years; age ≥25 may exclude younger adults who could benefit from screening based on risk factors)."]

["Minimum Age criteria specifies 60 years as the lower bound for colorectal cancer screening, which is inconsistent with major clinical guidelines (e.g., USPSTF recommends starting at age 45-50 for average-risk patients). The upper bound of 75 aligns with some guidelines that recommend stopping screening at 75, but the lower age threshold is clinically unreasonable."]

["In Demographic" age threshold of 30 years is not aligned with standard clinical guidelines (e.g., CDC recommends routine HIV screening for adults 15-64 years, with specific high-risk populations requiring earlier or more frequent screening). The 30-year cutoff may exclude younger adults who should be screened.]

[The "Is Obese" criterion uses a BMI threshold of 35 kg/m², which is higher than the standard clinical definition of obesity (BMI ≥30 kg/m²). This may exclude patients with BMI 30-34 who are also at risk. Additionally, the "In Demographic" age range (35-70 years) may be narrower than some guidelines, which often recommend screening starting at age 40 or lower for high-risk populations.]

["In Demographic" expression checks age >=30 instead of age >=21 as per USPSTF guidelines. The clinical rationale text correctly references 21-65 years, but the implementation logic starts at 30 years, excluding women aged 21-29 who should be screened annually or every 3 years depending on protocol.]

["In Demographic" condition uses age >=75 but USPSTF rationale references age >=65 for falls prevention recommendations. This creates a discrepancy between the demographic criteria (75+) and the cited clinical guideline (65+).]

["In Demographic condition incorrectly uses age >=25 instead of the USPSTF-recommended age >=18 for alcohol misuse screening eligibility. The rationale text correctly references 18 years, but the logic implementation has a conflicting value of 25."]

["In Demographic age range is 55-75 years instead of the USPSTF recommended 65-74 years for AAA screening eligibility. The Rationale also incorrectly states 65-75 years. This expands the target population beyond guideline-recommended parameters."]

[The MeetsInclusionCriteria logic incorrectly uses an OR operator between "Screening Age" (age ≥40) and "Gender" (female), which would include all female patients regardless of age. This should be an AND operator to ensure only female patients aged ≥40 are targeted for mammogram screening. Additionally, the "Mammogram" check correctly identifies absence of a procedure in the last year, but the flawed inclusion criteria may lead to inappropriate recommendations for females under 40.]

["Needs Tobacco Use Screening" expression does not include a check for the absence of recent screening (i.e., it only references "In Demographic" without incorporating "Has Recent Tobacco Screening"). This would result in recommending screening for all adults ≥18 years regardless of prior screening, which is inconsistent with clinical guidelines requiring periodic (e.g., annual) screening.]
[Missing upper age limit (USPSTF recommends 50-80 years), absence of pack-year history check (20 pack-years required), and no consideration of former smokers who quit within the past 15 years.]

["The 'ProcedureLookBack' function uses a lookback period of 1 month for colonoscopy screening, which is clinically unreasonable. Colonoscopies for average-risk colon cancer screening are typically recommended every 10 years, so the lookback period should be 10 years (e.g., 10 'year' units instead of 1 'month')."]

["Needs Falls Prevention Screening" incorrectly combines "In Demographic" (age ≥65) with "Has Recent Falls Assessment" (existing assessment in 1 year). Clinically, the recommendation should target patients who **do not** have a recent falls assessment. The "And" operator should be replaced with "Not" for "Has Recent Falls Assessment" to identify patients needing a new assessment. This logic inversion is critical for clinical appropriateness.]

["Needs Depression Screening condition incorrectly excludes patients with existing depression or bipolar diagnoses. USPSTF guidelines recommend screening the general adult population (age 18+), including those with a history of depression, to ensure ongoing monitoring and follow-up. Excluding patients with active diagnoses may lead to missed opportunities for re-evaluation and management adjustments."]
